summary:
The patient’s history is consistent with seasonal allergic conjunctivitis, characterized by itchy, watery eyes and recurrent episodes triggered by seasons. Combination antihistamine/mast cell stabilizing eyedrops—specifically ketotifen—are recommended as first-line therapy due to their effectiveness in controlling both acute itching and preventing future allergen-induced responses.  

(Note: Iron deficiency anemia and ankylosing spondylitis were noted but did not influence the diagnosis or treatment choice.)
